summary: Provision Simple Email Service (SESv2) for a new sending identity: a verified domain or address, plus an optional configuration set that governs how its sends are tracked and delivered. Both are engineer-gated creates — a verified identity can send mail on the account's behalf, so nothing here is blind self-service.

operations.title: Verify a new sending identity
operations.summary: Request verification of a new domain or email address for SES. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a new aws_sesv2_email_identity: a domain (verifies every address at that domain) or a specific email address, plus an optional configuration set to route its sending events through. DKIM signing and the DNS records needed to complete verification are always finished by the engineer. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: email_identity
params.label: Domain or email address
params.help: A domain (e.g. example.com) verifies every address at that domain; a specific email address (e.g. no-reply@example.com) verifies only that one address.

params.name: configuration_set
params.label: Configuration set
params.help: Optional. Routes this identity's sending events through a specific configuration set. Create one first (ses-configuration-set-create) or leave blank to use the account default.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Identity name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this identity sends
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this identity.

decisions: DKIM signing (dkim_signing_attributes — BYODKIM or Easy DKIM) is an engineer follow-up; without it this identity still verifies, but sends without a custom DKIM key
A domain identity also needs its DNS verification/DKIM CNAME records published in the zone before mail sends successfully — the engineer confirms who controls that zone

operations.title: Create a new configuration set
operations.summary: Request a new SES configuration set for sending and delivery options. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Specify a new aws_sesv2_configuration_set: whether sending starts enabled, whether reputation metrics publish to CloudWatch, and the TLS policy for delivery. Event destinations (SNS/CloudWatch/Kinesis Firehose) and a dedicated IP pool are always finished by the engineer. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: sending_enabled
params.label: Sending enabled
params.help: Off pauses every send that uses this configuration set, without deleting it.

params.name: reputation_metrics_enabled
params.label: Publish reputation metrics
params.help: Publishes bounce/complaint reputation metrics for this configuration set to CloudWatch.

params.name: tls_policy
params.label: TLS policy
params.help: REQUIRE refuses delivery over a connection that isn't TLS-encrypted.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Configuration set name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this configuration set is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this configuration set.

decisions: Event destinations (publishing sends/bounces/complaints to SNS, CloudWatch, or Kinesis Firehose) are engineer follow-ups — this form provisions the configuration set with none wired yet

Suppression list behavior (suppressed_reasons), a custom click-tracking redirect domain, and VDM (engagement/guardian) dashboard options keep SES account defaults unless an engineer sets them explicitly
A dedicated IP pool (sending_pool_name) is an engineer decision — omitted here uses the shared SES IP pool
